16.kj: adversary, chide, complain, contend, debate, [idiom] ever, [idiom] lay wait, plead, rebuke, strive, [idiom] thoroughly.

20.gl: a sea (as breaking in noisy surf) or large body of water; specifically (with the article), the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river, or an artifical basin; locally, the west, or (rarely) the south
20.dv: from an unused root meaning to roar;

20.gl: to be able, literally (can, could) or morally (may, might)
20.kj: be able, any at all (ways), attain, can (away with, (-not)), could, endure, might, overcome, have power, prevail, still, suffer.

20.dv: from an unused root meaning apparently to be sticky (rather perb. a demonstrative); from H2894 (טוּא), through the idea of dirt to be swept away);

20.gl: to drive out from a possession; especially to expatriate or divorce
20.kj: cast up (out), divorced (woman), drive away (forth, out), expel, [idiom] surely put away, trouble, thrust out.

20.gl: water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen
20.dv: dual of a primitive noun (but used in a singular sense);

20.kj: appease, idleness, (at, be at, be in, give) quiet(-ness), (be at, be in, give, have, take) rest, settle, be still.

21.gl: a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle

21.dv: as if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist;

21.gl: morally wrong; concretely, an (actively) bad person
21.kj: [phrase] condemned, guilty, ungodly, wicked (man), that did wrong.
